title: [REQ] Add EQP functional area to REQUIREMENTS.md
body:
## Objective
Define requirements for the generic Equipment contract. Every equipment model must satisfy these regardless of type.

## Requirements to add
- EQP-001: Port declaration before initialise()
- EQP-002: No write_port() on IN ports
- EQP-003: No read from undeclared ports
- EQP-004: on_tick() reads CommandStore into IN ports before do_step()
- EQP-005: on_tick() writes OUT ports to ParameterStore after do_step()
- EQP-006: on_tick() calls publish_ready() after ParameterStore writes
- EQP-007: FmuEquipment maps FMU variable names via parameter_map
- EQP-008: FmuEquipment applies IN ports to FMU inputs before doStep()
- EQP-009: FmuEquipment reads FMU outputs into OUT ports after doStep()
- EQP-010: NativeEquipment calls step_fn with (equipment, t, dt)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All EQP requirements added to REQUIREMENTS.md
- Traceability index updated
- Status: BASELINED

title: [REQ] Add EPS functional area to REQUIREMENTS.md
body:
## Objective
Define requirements for the EPS spacecraft models (SolarArray, Battery, PCDU, integrated and decomposed).

## Requirements to add
- EPS-001: SolarArray power proportional to illumination
- EPS-002: SolarArray zero power in eclipse
- EPS-003: Battery SoC decreases when charge_current negative
- EPS-004: Battery SoC increases when charge_current positive
- EPS-005: Battery voltage follows non-linear SoC curve (3.0V-4.2V)
- EPS-006: PCDU positive charge_current when generation exceeds load
- EPS-007: PCDU negative charge_current when load exceeds generation
- EPS-008: Integrated EPS charges battery in full sunlight
- EPS-009: Integrated EPS discharges battery in eclipse
- EPS-010: Decomposed EPS matches integrated EPS behaviour

## Acceptance Criteria
- All EPS requirements added to REQUIREMENTS.md
- Traceability index updated
- Status: BASELINED

title: [REQ] Restructure test directory to unit/equipment/integration/spacecraft
body:
## Objective
Reorganise the test suite to clearly separate unit, equipment contract, integration, and spacecraft model tests.

## Target structure
```
tests/
  unit/               - SVF platform classes in isolation
  equipment/          - Generic Equipment contract (EQP-xxx)
  integration/        - SVF infrastructure mechanics
  spacecraft/         - Specific model behaviour (EPS-xxx etc.)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests moved to correct directory
- No test exists without a @pytest.mark.requirement() tag
- No redundant tests
- All tests pass after restructuring
- CI green

## Implements
SVF-DEV-072

title: [REQ] Ensure every test is traced to a requirement
body:
## Objective
Audit every test in the suite. Every test must reference at least one requirement via @pytest.mark.requirement(). Tests that cannot be traced to a requirement must be deleted or a requirement must be created for them.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every test has @pytest.mark.requirement(...) marker
- No requirement exists without at least one test
- No test exists without a corresponding requirement
- Traceability matrix generated from pytest markers
- CI verifies marker presence (warn on missing)

## Implements
SVF-DEV-072, SVF-DEV-073

title: [REQ] Generate requirements traceability matrix from pytest markers
body:
## Objective
Implement a pytest plugin hook that collects all @pytest.mark.requirement() markers and generates a traceability matrix after each test run.

## Output format
```
Requirement | Test Cases              | Verdict
EQP-001     | test_port_declaration   | PASS
EPS-001     | test_solar_proportional | PASS
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Traceability matrix written to results/traceability.txt after each run
- Matrix included in JUnit XML output as custom property
- Requirements with no tests flagged as UNTESTED
- Implements SVF-DEV-073

## Implements
SVF-DEV-072, SVF-DEV-073
